_comment: Global ingredient substitute groups. Unlike barAliases (exact synonyms), these are 'close enough' swaps where the drink changes but still works. Used by barMatch.js to expand shelf matching. Per-recipe substitutes (on individual drink ingredients) override these.

substitutes.note: Flavor changes but acid ratio works 1:1
Both are sweeter citrus; adjust sweetener to taste
Bourbon sweeter, rye spicier; both work in most whiskey cocktails
Gold adds slight caramel; works in most rum cocktails
Mezcal adds smokiness; split or sub in most tequila drinks
Both oak-aged; anejo is richer but works in stirred drinks
All provide sweetness at similar concentration; flavor profile shifts
Deep/caramel sweeteners; use same quantity
Cointreau is drier, Grand Marnier is richer; all orange-forward
Amaretto is almond, Frangelico is hazelnut; both sweet and nutty
Complex herbal profiles; not identical but similar role in cocktails
Mr Black is drier; all provide coffee flavor
Lillet is sweeter; works as a lighter sub in martini-style drinks
Cocchi lighter, Punt e Mes more bitter; similar role in stirred drinks
Angostura is the standard; any aromatic bitter works similarly
All add citrus complexity as a supporting dash
Richness decreases; texture changes but role is the same
Vegan swaps; coconut cream closest to heavy cream in body
Aquafaba is vegan; same frothy texture in sours
Tonic is bitter/sweet, soda is neutral; only sometimes interchangeable
Ginger beer is spicier and stronger; ginger ale is milder
All bring depth and warmth; spiced rum adds vanilla/cinnamon
Plymouth is softer; both work in classic gin cocktails
All cherry-forward; maraschino is drier, heering is sweeter
All anise-flavored; used as rinse or dash in Sazerac-style drinks
